title: hypericum scouleri subsp. nortoniae
intro: Hypericum scouleri subsp. nortoniae, also known as Nortonia's St. John's Wort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the St. John's Wort family, characterized by its yellow or greenish-yellow flowers and woody stems. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been traditionally used to treat wounds, skin conditions, and minor infections. The health benefits of Hypericum scouleri subsp. nortoniae include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable remedy for various health issues. In herbal preparations, the plant's aerial parts are typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support skin health, reduce stress, and promote overall well-being.
